page.title: Frontends
page.description: One row per HAProxy frontend section. Drag rows to reorder; HAProxy parses sections top-to-bottom.

actions.cloneTitle: Duplicate this frontend with a fresh id + section name

frontend.filterPlaceholder: Filter by name, id, mode, bind address…
frontend.empty: No frontends. Click Add frontend to create one.
frontend.emptyFiltered: No frontends match the current filter.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete frontend?
deleteConfirm.note: This change applies immediately on save.

errors.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)
errors.nameFormat: name must be a valid HAProxy section identifier (letters/digits/_/-, start with letter)
errors.defaultsRequired: a defaults block must be selected (create one on the Defaults page first)
errors.bindRequired: at least one bind is required

edit.idHelp: Immutable after creation. Lowercase a-z, digits, _ or -, start with a letter.
edit.maxconnHelp: Per-frontend session cap. Leave blank for default.
edit.fromDefaultsHelp: Required. Each frontend inherits from one named defaults block. Define them on the Defaults page.
edit.errorFilesHelp: Reference one of the `http-errors NAME` sections (define them on the Error pages tab).
edit.noneErrorFiles: none — use defaults or per-status overrides

bind.label: label (UX-only)
bind.sslEnabled: SSL / TLS enabled on this bind
bind.quicCcAlgoHelp: Congestion control for this listener. `nocc` disables CC (debug only).
bind.quicSocketHelp: `connection` = one UDP socket per QUIC connection (better perf, requires SO_REUSEPORT).
bind.minTitle: A frontend needs at least one bind

ssl.noCas: No trusted CAs uploaded yet — add one on the Certificates page.
ssl.caFileHelp: Bundle used to verify client certs presented to this bind.
ssl.caVerifyHelp: Optional. Lets HAProxy advertise different CA names than the chain it verifies against.
ssl.noCrls: No trusted CRLs uploaded yet — add one on the Certificates page.
ssl.crlHelp: CRL HAProxy uses to reject revoked client certs at the TLS handshake.

sslFlags.strictSni: strict-sni (reject TLS with no matching cert)

backend.filterPlaceholder: Filter by name, id, mode, server…

actions.cloneTitle: Duplicate this backend with a fresh id/name. Useful when several vhosts share the same upstream pool but you want per-vhost stats rows.
actions.inUseTitle: In use by at least one rule, default_backend, or SNI mapping; remove the reference first.

backend.emptyFiltered: No backends match the current filter.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete backend?
deleteConfirm.note: This change applies immediately on save.

errors.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)
errors.minServers: at least one server is required

servers.title: Servers
servers.help: Order matters when balance is static-rr or first, and for the failover sequence among backup servers. Drag rows, use the arrows, or click a position badge to jump to a specific slot. Sorting by a column hides the drag handles — click the position header to clear the sort.
servers.filter: Filter servers by name or address…
servers.emptyFiltered: No servers match the current filter.
servers.minServerTitle: A backend needs at least one server.
servers.name: Name
servers.sniTitle: Optional. ssl_fc_sni forwards the inbound SNI verbatim; a literal hostname overrides it.
servers.caFileTitle: Trusted CA bundle for upstream TLS verification. Only relevant when SSL verify is required.

page.title: ACLs
page.description: Reusable named matchers. Reference them by name from any rule condition; the renderer emits an acl NAME … line at the top of every frontend body that uses it.

acl.empty: No ACLs yet. Add one before referencing it from a rule.
acl.emptyFiltered: No ACLs match the current filter.

actions.deleteBlocked: Referenced by {{count}} rule(s); detach them first.

errors.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)
errors.nameFormat: name must be a valid HAProxy ACL identifier (letter-start, letters/digits/_/./-)

edit.idHelp: Internal id (immutable). Lowercase a-z, digits, _ or -, start with letter.
edit.name: HAProxy ACL name
edit.nameHelp: Used in rule conditions to reference this ACL.
edit.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Matches all requests to home.example.com
edit.fieldCurrent: (currently set; type custom in field below)
edit.values: Values (one or more, space-separated when rendered)

page.title: Rules
page.description: Per-frontend rule chains, one tab per HAProxy phase. Drag rows to reorder. Phase order left-to-right matches HAProxy's evaluation order.

rule.noFrontendsNotice: No frontends defined yet. Create a frontend before adding rules.
rule.empty: No rules in this phase. Click Add rule to create one.
rule.emptyFiltered: No rules match the current filter.
rule.evaluationNote: Rules evaluate top-to-bottom. First terminating match wins for actions like deny, redirect, use-backend. Non-terminating actions (set-header, set-var) all run in order.

errors.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)

action.heading: Action
action.name: Name
action.securityProfileHelp: Sugar action — expands at render-time into the profile's deny + track-sc chain gated on this rule's condition.
action.authProviderHelp: Sugar action — expands at render-time into the provider's auth chain gated on this rule's condition.

condition.heading: Condition
condition.note: (empty = always; terms join with AND by default, switch to OR per row)

page.title: Routes
page.description1: A "route" here is a pair: an hdr(host) ACL + an http-request use-backend rule that references it. Use + New route to create both via a wizard. Edits to the underlying ACL or Rule happen on the

route.empty: No routes derived. Either no use-backend rules exist yet, or no host-matching ACLs are referenced by any rule. Click + New route to create the first pair, or build the primitives directly from the ACLs and Rules pages.
route.openInNewTab: Open https://{{host}}/ in a new tab

routeWizard.noHttpFrontends: No HTTP frontends defined. Create one on the Frontends page first.
routeWizard.hostnamesHelp: One or more hostnames matched by hdr(host) -i. Wildcards allowed (-i default).
routeWizard.noBackends: No backends defined. Create one on the Backends page first.
routeWizard.aclIdHelp: Internal id for the host-matching ACL entity.
routeWizard.diffNote: Saving applies through the normal state → render → reload pipeline. The ACL and Rule are afterward fully editable from the ACLs and Rules pages.

page.title: Defaults blocks
page.description: Named defaults NAME { … } sections. Each frontend picks one via from. HAProxy 2.4+.

defaults.empty: No defaults blocks yet. You need at least one before adding any frontend.
defaults.emptyFiltered: No defaults blocks match the current filter.

actions.deleteBlocked: Referenced by {{count}} frontend(s); change them first.

errors.idFormat: id must match a-z, 0-9, _, - (starting with a letter)
errors.nameFormat: name must be a valid HAProxy section identifier (letters/digits/_/-, start with letter)
errors.sectionHelp: References one of the named http-errors sections (top-level).

edit.initAddrHelp: Cmd/Ctrl-click to select multiple. Empty omits the directive.

advanced.label: Advanced HAProxy directives
advanced.help: Appended verbatim inside the defaults block.

globalSettings.title: HAProxy global block
globalSettings.description: Capacity, logging, and unique-id format. These render into the global section of haproxy.cfg. SSL / TLS, Lua plugins, QUIC tunables, and raw passthrough directives are configured in the cards below.
globalSettings.maxconnHelp: Per-process simultaneous connection limit. Effective value capped by fd-hard-limit / 2 - 256.
globalSettings.fdHardLimitHelp: Hard ceiling on file descriptors HAProxy will ever request from the OS.
globalSettings.bufsizeHelp: Per-stream buffer size in bytes. 64K covers most workloads.
globalSettings.hardStopAfterHelp: Grace period for old workers to drain after a reload before SIGKILL.
globalSettings.logLevelHelp: HAProxy log emit level (passed to log stdout format raw local0 X).
globalSettings.uniqueIdHeaderHelp: Header name used to surface the unique request id. Blank disables the directive.
globalSettings.uniqueIdFormatHelp: HAProxy log-format expression. Default %{+X}o\ %ci:%cp_%fi:%fp_%Ts_%rt:%pid is the canonical hex form per docs 8.2.1.

advancedDirectives.title: Advanced global directives
advancedDirectives.description: Raw passthrough lines appended verbatim to the rendered global section. One directive per row. Use only for HAProxy features patchpanel doesn't model natively — these lines bypass schema validation.

page.title: Advanced — HAProxy infrastructure
page.description: DNS resolvers, peer-sync stick-table groups, SMTP mailer groups for email-alert, log-ring sinks, and reusable security policy registry (rate-limit / geo-block / bot-defense). Cert-related infrastructure lives on the Certificates tab; error-page sections live on the Error pages tab; additional listeners live on the new Frontends tab.

quic.title: QUIC tunables (global)
quic.description: Process-wide and per-side QUIC knobs rendered as tune.quic.* directives in the global section. Per-bind QUIC keywords (quic-cc-algo, quic-force-retry, quic-socket) are configured per-frontend on each quic4@ / quic6@ bind. Backend QUIC requires a server address prefixed with quic4@ or quic6@.
quic.maxIdleTimeoutHelp: QUIC idle timeout on this side.
quic.listenHelp: Master switch for QUIC listeners. Replaces the legacy no-quic global flag.
quic.memTxMaxHelp: Process-wide TX memory ceiling for QUIC.
quic.zeroCopyHelp: Use zero-copy egress forwarding when the kernel supports it.

ssl.title: SSL / TLS Globals
ssl.description: Mozilla profile + per-cipher/curve/suite overrides. Frontend and Backend are configured here. Empty array on any field means HAProxy will not emit the corresponding ssl-default-* directive — OpenSSL built-in defaults apply at runtime.
ssl.presetStale: The {{name}} preset was based on version {{old}} when last saved. Current preset version is {{current}}.
ssl.resetBindTitle: Drop any bind-side override that already matches the preset value
ssl.resetServerTitle: Drop any server-side override that already matches the preset value
ssl.profileHelpPart2: custom = no preset overlay; every field is explicit.
ssl.noCapability: No items reported by this HAProxy / OpenSSL build for this field.
ssl.emptyWarning: No items selected — HAProxy will not emit a ssl-default-* directive for this field. OpenSSL's built-in defaults will apply at runtime.
ssl.probing: Probing installed HAProxy + OpenSSL for available ciphers / curves / sigalgs…
ssl.probeFailedNote: The cipher / curve / sigalg checkbox lists fall back to whatever is already in state. You can still save.
ssl.keylogOnBody: Session keys are written to the HAProxy log, allowing anyone with log access to decrypt past TLS traffic captured on the wire. Turn off before going live; this is a debugging-only flag.

keylogConfirm.title: Enable tune.ssl.keylog?
keylogConfirm.body: Turning tune.ssl.keylog on writes session keys to the HAProxy log. Anyone with log access can decrypt TLS traffic captured on the wire. Use only for short-lived Wireshark debugging on a controlled environment. Never leave on in production.

tune.dhParamHelp: DH params bit size. 2048 min; 4096 default.

ssl.providersHelp: Tracked in state for documentation. Actual provider availability is reported by openssl list -providers and shown read-only above this card.
ssl.extraFilesHelp: Extra cert sidecar files to load alongside each PEM. Common values: ocsp, sctl, issuer.

prompts.custom: Enter a bind address (hostname, IP, or HAProxy address form):

warnings.unreachable: This address isn't on any local interface and doesn't match any VIP. HAProxy will fail to bind unless transparent is set in this bind's options and net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind=1 is set on the host. patchpanel will not configure either for you.
warnings.floating: This is a floating IP managed by keepalived. To make HAProxy start even when this node isn't holding the VIP, add transparent to bindOptions and set net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind=1 on the host. patchpanel will not do either for you.
warnings.nodeLocal: This address only exists on this node — other cluster nodes will fail to bind it.

entitySection.deleteNote: This change applies immediately on save.

stop.confirmRequired: Stopping HAProxy requires { "confirm": true } in the body — this will drop all proxied connections

server.invalidState: Server state must be one of: {{allowed}}
server.invalidWeight: Server weight must be an integer between 0 and 256

master.timeout: HAProxy master socket timed out after {{timeoutMs}}ms

stats.timeout: HAProxy stats socket timed out after {{timeoutMs}}ms

control.pidUnreadable: Could not read HAProxy pid from {{path}}
control.directStartUnavailable: Direct control strategy cannot start HAProxy — no supervisor configured. Set HAPROXY_CONTROL_STRATEGY to s6 or systemd, or start HAProxy manually.
